palette_hint: Background #F4F6FA (light steel), body text #1C2333 (near-black navy), accent #2563EB (electric blue) and #E84545 (alert red) for highlights and badges.
type_style: Headings in system-ui bold 700, body and table cells in 'Courier New' monospace for numeric columns and Georgia serif for prose, creating a clinical data-report feel.
description: Vaultline is a fictional enterprise cloud-storage comparison site that lets IT buyers pit five storage tiers—Starter, Business, Scale, Enterprise, and Sovereign—against each other across dozens of technical and commercial criteria.
constraints: All comparison tables must have sticky <thead> (position: sticky; top: 0) with background #1C2333 and white text, zebra striping on rows using :nth-child(even) background #E8ECF5, and row hover highlight #D0DAEF.
Numeric data columns (GB limits, IOPS, price) must be right-aligned and rendered in 'Courier New' monospace at 13px; text descriptor columns are left-aligned in Georgia 14px.

The custom-quote page must use a multi-column form layout: two columns at 480px each with a 24px gutter, max-width 1040px centered; all inputs and selects share border: 1.5px solid #94A3B8, border-radius: 4px, padding: 8px 12px, and focus state border-color: #2563EB with outline: none.

Custom CSS-only checkboxes and radio buttons: hidden native inputs, visible 18x18px styled boxes/circles using a sibling <label> with ::before pseudo-element; checked state fills #2563EB with a white checkmark or dot via content and color.

Badge pills for plan tiers use inline-block elements: background #2563EB text white for recommended, background #E84545 text white for deprecated, background #64748B text white for legacy; border-radius: 99px; padding: 2px 10px; font-size: 11px uppercase.

page_specs.plans: Displays a full-width sticky-header comparison table with all five Vaultline plans as columns and 18 rows of criteria including monthly price (USD), storage cap (TB), egress fee ($/GB), SLA uptime (%), max file size (GB), and support tier. Each plan column header contains its badge pill and a bolded price. The recommended 'Scale' plan column has a 2px solid #2563EB left and right border to visually frame it.
page_specs.features: Two stacked comparison tables: the first covers security features (AES-256 at rest, TLS 1.3 in transit, WORM compliance, SSO support, audit log retention in days) and the second covers performance features (max concurrent uploads, IOPS guarantee, CDN edge nodes, API rate limit per minute, SDK languages). Rows use zebra striping; boolean values render as a filled circle (#2563EB) for yes and an em-dash in #94A3B8 for no, styled with CSS classes.
page_specs.benchmark-data: Three dense numeric tables presenting third-party benchmark results: sequential read/write throughput (MB/s), random 4K IOPS, and time-to-first-byte latency (ms) across five test regions (US-East, EU-West, AP-South, SA-East, AU-Sydney). All figures right-aligned monospace; the top-scoring cell per row is highlighted with background #DBEAFE and font-weight 700. A short one-paragraph methodology note sits above each table in Georgia 14px.
page_specs.custom-quote: A two-column form (max-width 1040px, 24px gutter) where buyers configure a custom Vaultline deployment. Fields include: company name, contact email, estimated monthly storage (TB) via number input, primary region (select dropdown with 8 options), required SLA (radio group: 99.9% / 99.95% / 99.99%), add-on features (CSS-only checkboxes: Dedicated Egress IP, WORM Vault, 24/7 Phone Support, Custom Domain CDN, HIPAA BAA), and a textarea for special requirements. Submit button is full-width below the columns, background #2563EB, white text, border-radius 4px, padding 14px. Required fields show a red 1.5px border on :invalid after :focus.
page_specs.faq: A two-column layout (left: 280px sticky nav listing 12 FAQ categories in a bordered list; right: scrollable content area) presenting 24 question-and-answer pairs grouped under headings like Billing, Data Residency, Security Compliance, and Migration. Each Q uses font-weight 700 system-ui 15px and each A uses Georgia 14px line-height 1.7. A small inline comparison table inside the 'Billing' section shows overage rates per plan (6 rows × 5 columns) with the same zebra and sticky-header treatment as the main plans table.
